latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 15:18
RNS Number : 7222T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
19 February 2026
19 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of equity
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that, further to the Company's circular dated 5 November 2025 and the authorities approved by shareholders of the Company at the general meeting held on 1 December 2025, application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 1,408,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission relates to the allotment of 1,408,000 new Ordinary Shares ("
New Shares
") to be issued in terms of a historic settlement agreement.
Admission of the New Shares is expected to occur on 20 February 2026. The New Shares will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 30,532,930 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 30,532,930. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Update on Proposed Acquisition
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0418T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Proposed Acquisition
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company is pleased to provide an update
on the Company's proposed acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
").
As detailed in the Company's announcement on 22 December 2025, the Company engaged SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd ("
SLR
"), to conduct a confirmatory drilling campaign, commencing on Lots 7 and 8 at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia, with the intention of proving a measured resource, and a technical memorandum has been provided by SLR. A total of 26 drillholes have now been completed using three drilling methods, diamond, percussion and blast hole, for a total of 1,265 metres.
From the samples collected during the drilling programme Quantitative X-Ray Diffraction (QXRD) and clay mineralogical analyses will be undertaken to characterise material properties relevant to assessing pozzolanic potential. Drill core pieces representing all lithological units were also selected for petrographic examination and X-ray Fluorescence (XRF) analysis to document mineralogical composition and textural features.
Initial volumes pending validation of drilling data, interpretation, geological modelling and material test results within Lots 7 and 8 indicate that the concept pit (see figure 1 below) covered by the areas of Lots 7 and 8 contains a total of
86.5 million tonnes of pozzolanic sandstone from a total volume of 151.75 million tonnes of material. Whilst these initial volumes are being assessed for resource status pending material testing and validation, the purpose of the update is to increase confidence in the volumes towards measured status. Volumes may vary based on further development of the geological model and validation.
Atlas Metals is expecting SLR to complete the updated Competent Person's Report ("
CPR
"), using data obtained from this drilling programme, which is expected to include certain JORC resources being upgraded from the inferred to measured category, prior to the completion of the Proposed Acquisition. The last published CPR on the Yammacoona Sand Quarry, as announced by the Company on 28 November 2025, may be found on the Company's website at:
https://atlasmetalsgroup.com/investors/reports
The Proposed Acquisition continues to progress in line with the Board's expectations and further announcements on the progress of the Proposed Acquisition will be made when appropriate.
Figure 1 - Concept Pit Design, showing the locations of blocks 7 and 8 (from SLR CPR 26 November 2025
Illustration: Recent SLR drilling on behalf of the Company at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia

latest.title: Update on Kyrgyz Uranium Asset
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1437S
Atlas Metals Group PLC
09 February 2026
9 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on
Kyrgyz
Uranium Asset
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, is pleased to provide an update on the Company's interest in the Kamushanovskoye uranium deposit, located in northern Kyrgyzstan, 48km northeast of the capital of the Kyrgyz Republic, Bishkek (the "
Project
"). The Project is owned by International Mining Company Invest Inc. ("
IMC
"), a US based company, and currently Atlas Metals has a 10 % interest in IMC.
Whilst the Company's primary focus remains on the proposed
acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
"), which is progressing well,
this update is provided in view of the significant increase in the uranium price since the Company invested in the Project in 2018. At that time, the in-situ value of contained uranium totalled US$151 million at a uranium price of US$27-28/lb. Over the last six months, the uranium price has reached US$80-85/lb, placing a value of approximately US$700 million on the contained resources.
The Project has been under exploration since 2011 and the State Reserve Committee of Kyrgyzstan ("
GKZ
") granted an application for a mining licence in January 2019 (the "
Mining Licence
") for 3,371.1 tonnes of uranium reserves (8.731 million lbs U3O8). This provides an in-situ value of approximately US$700 million at a uranium price of US$80-85/lb. In May 2019, the Kyrgyz parliament voted to ban uranium mining in the country and the Mining Licence was cancelled without any compensation. IMC contested this action and appealed the decision under international arbitration. Hearings were held in 2024-25 and a decision is in process (case ICSID ARB/22/25). The Company believes IMC's case is strengthened by the Parliament of Kyrgyzstan approving a bill lifting the ban on prospecting, exploration, development and mining of uranium and thorium in the Kyrgyz Republic in June 2024.
As part of the work completed since the grant of the Mining Licence and to support IMC's case at arbitration, a detailed review and assessment of the Project took place and was completed in 2024. The review was completed by Dr Mike Armitage, BSc, MIMMM, FGS, CEng, CGeol and confirmed both the potential viability of the extraction method as well as the resource estimate approved by GKZ.
The deposit is uranium oxide hosted in peat soils at depths from surface down to a maximum of 14 metres. Testwork on site of in-situ recovery (ISR) confirmed good extraction of uranium using sodium carbonate as the leaching solution. ISR comprises the injection of the leaching solution into the site and recovering the pregnant solution using shallow pump wells. The pregnant solution can be enhanced on site to a product than can be shipped for final processing. Due to the shallow depths and simple technology, the operating and capital costs are low.
Discussions are underway with possible development partners and the Kyrgyz government to find a satisfactory conclusion for all parties.
Chris Chadwick, Chief Executive Officer of Atlas Metals, commented:
"While our primary focus is on completing the transformative acquisition of UPSA, it is noteworthy that our long-term belief in Atlas'
Kyrgyz uranium
project is being validated. We will continue to seek to derive value from this project in parallel with completing the UPSA transaction and I look forward to providing further updates in due course."
For further information, please contact:
Atlas Metals Group plc:
Christopher Chadwick
+44 (0) 20 7796 9060

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG Ltd is a small‑cap company that owns and develops uranium assets in Kyrgyzstan, mainly through a stake in International Mining Company Invest Inc. The focus is on exploration, development and the potential production of uranium for nuclear power.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a critical raw material for nuclear reactors, and demand is expected to grow as countries look for low‑carbon energy. AMG’s asset in Kyrgyzstan gives it access to a sizeable uranium reserve that could become a significant supplier in the region.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has been trading at a very low valuation and is in a deep sell‑off. It recently raised capital through an equity issue, announced an ATM facility, and is in talks about a possible acquisition. The company is still in the early stages of turning its asset into a cash‑generating operation.

rns_narrative: Capital‑raising activity – Equity issue and ATM facility show that the company is actively seeking liquidity to fund exploration and development.
Asset‑level updates – The latest news on the Kyrgyz uranium asset and the proposed acquisition signal that management is working to unlock value in the reserve.
Corporate governance and ownership – Recent holdings and director dealings suggest that insiders are still invested, which can be a positive sign for long‑term stability.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – The community is mostly silent, with only a handful of bullish posts hinting at a potential rebound but no widespread chatter.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the 10% stake in a $700m asset and the possibility of a deal that could lift the share price.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter focuses on the lack of production, the uncertainty of the acquisition, and the risk that the equity issue could dilute existing shareholders.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment remains muted; the few bullish comments have not yet translated into broader market interest, keeping the mood neutral to slightly bullish.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest, which means the market is largely unaware – a perfect set‑up for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the equity issue or a follow‑up funding round that provides the cash needed for drilling and development.
A formal announcement of the proposed acquisition or a definitive agreement that would bring in additional capital and expertise.
Positive exploration results from the Kyrgyz uranium asset, such as a higher grade or a larger resource estimate.
A regulatory or licensing decision that clears the way for production in Kyrgyzstan.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include a sharp uptick in daily volume, a break above the short‑term moving average, and any press release confirming the acquisition or a new funding round. A sudden spike in insider buying would also be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash; capitulation may already be complete, but the mean‑reversion engine is waiting on a catalyst. A timely announcement or positive exploration data could trigger the bounce within the next 2‑3 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep plunge has created a large upside window. Historical data shows that a 70–95% drop can produce 40–60% average returns once a catalyst arrives. If the company unlocks its asset and the share price moves to the midpoint of the 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 50–70% from current levels. The acquisition or funding event would provide the capital and confidence needed for a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the acquisition falls through or the equity issue fails, the company may struggle to fund exploration, pushing the price back down. A lack of production and regulatory hurdles could also keep the share stuck in a low‑valuation zone. Worst‑case, the stock could become worthless if the asset is deemed non‑viable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is capped at the current price; upside potential could be 3–5 times the entry level, creating a highly favorable asymmetry.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip: wait for a clear volume spike and a move above the 20‑day moving average. Size the position so that a 10% drop would trigger a stop at 10% below entry, keeping risk low. Hold for 6–12 months, looking for signs of production or a licensing decision.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger: A press release confirming the acquisition or a new funding round, or a sudden volume surge of >20% above average. Also watch for insider buying of >5% of shares.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk identified, so no passing recommendation. The setup is still contrarian bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash has left AMG in a prime contrarian position; a timely acquisition or funding event could unleash a 50–70% rebound, while the risk is limited to the current price.
